metadata.title: Why the Agent That Can Do More Can Go Wrong More
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of claude-agentic-ai candidate 06. Action surface and blast radius mechanism only — what the agent can touch is determined by access granted, not by task description.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL #1F6F5C = narrow surface / contained blast radius / safe; CRIMSON #BF3339 = broad surface / overreach / irreversible; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO access-ladder enumeration in full (seven rungs); NO OWASP LLM Top 10 history; NO prompt injection attack mechanics.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: You give the agent access to your working directory to organize some files. You didn't give it permission to delete your client contracts — but you didn't say it couldn't.
beats.scene_description: a computer desktop with a folder open, many document types visible — editorial collage, newsprint texture, no faces

beats.narration_text: Within minutes, files are sorted, renamed, some deleted as duplicates. The tax documents remain. The client contracts do not. No malice. No ordinary error.
beats.scene_description: large folder square with mixed file types — contracts turn CRIMSON and disappear, tax docs stay TEAL, rename/sort actions shown

beats.narration_text: An agent given a cleanup task should only touch clutter. Here is the case where it deleted irreplaceable contracts. Why?
beats.scene_description: question card — CARD copy: Why did a cleanup task delete irreplaceable contracts?

beats.narration_text: The action surface is everything the agent can touch if its reasoning leads it there. It is determined by the access you grant — not by the task description. The task was cleanup. The surface was the whole directory.
beats.scene_description: large bounded square (action surface) labeled with all contents; task description label (cleanup) much smaller inside — mismatch shown

beats.narration_text: Blast radius scales with the action surface, not with the intended task.
beats.scene_description: section card — CARD copy: Blast radius scales with the surface, not the task.

beats.narration_text: Tom grants folder-level access to run a report. The folder contains a secrets subfolder with API keys. The task is read-only summarization. The agent reads the secrets file for context. The secrets travel into the session log Tom pastes into Slack.
beats.scene_description: folder tree: report folder + secrets subfolder — agent reads both, CRIMSON path shows secrets flowing into session log into Slack

beats.narration_text: The fix is to draw the surface before the agent starts. Grant only the minimum access the task requires. Keep source files in their original location. Copy only what the agent needs into a dedicated working folder.
beats.scene_description: two columns: broad surface (CRIMSON, full directory) vs narrow surface (TEAL, working folder with copies only); blast radius circles show different sizes

beats.narration_text: Before any agentic task: name the minimum access the task requires. Keep sensitive data outside the working scope. If the agent makes an error, can it be undone? Draw the boundary. Then start.
beats.scene_description: three-step practice: name minimum access → exclude sensitive data → confirm reversibility; each step appears as TEAL chip

beats.narration_text: The action surface is the boundary you draw. Draw it before the agent starts.
beats.scene_description: endcard — CARD copy: Draw the boundary before the agent starts. / sub: AGENTIC AI
